review_text: Cute little camp site around 15m from town. The camp site is set beside a river, ideal to wake up in the mornings and see nature come to life. Across the road is a beautiful beach and Apollo Bay Riding club.
Super comfy tent, so cozy, free BBQ though no utensils or cutlery provided

Spent a comfortable and cozy night in a riverside eco tent. Next time we will try the river haven tent with an ensuite as the only detraction was the track to the amenities block. Friendly and clean park
Full Moon 🌝

We were booked to stay here over the weekend however due to a change of plans we needed to change our booking, despite being outside of the no cancellation period I couldn’t get onto the park despite calling multiple times. When they finally responded via email the customer service was blunt and unapologetic, and they gave us zero options for potentially moving our booking to a different weekend. Was looking forward to staying here but will think twice before booking here again.
Amazing place to.stay, sea, river and mountains all visible from camp site
Very nice park well maintained amenities are good rather popular it seems.. we will stay again on our way home. Appolo Bay is a lovely township has most things you need very popular with tourists.
Nice park but it needs upgrading toilets and shower blocks
The tents were very comfortable and facilities convenient.  The location was fantastic.
Really cute campsite. Clean toilets, showers & kitchen. The sites by the river are very picturesque.
Great location and peaceful position by the river Thankyou we enjoyed our stay

Always camped here before the renovation took place and loved it.

The new version is good. The river haven tents are nice although a huge loss of traditional camping vibe in the unpowered sites section due to the new glamping styled tents there instead. How ever this camp site is still awesome.

The only thing I didn’t feel comfortable with is campsite R22 is situated directly under power lines and a power box on a power poll not far away with extreme hissing and power surging noises at times. You also get the view of power lines over the river at R22. Given the fact R22 is a long site I ended up moving north on R22 to avoid being directly under the power lines and away from the surging sound.

Will continue to camp here just not R22.

Well done on the Reno.

Wir hatten ein Eco Tent ohne Badezimmer. Wir konnten den Schlüssel super einfach abholen und es war alles beschriftet. Die Badezimmer waren super sauber, ordentlich und reichlich vorhanden. Die Küche war auch top! Alles was man benötigt. Mikrowelle, Toaster, Wasserkocher. Sogar Kaffee und Tee gab es dort.
Und die Aussicht! Ein Traum! Danke für diese tolle Unterkunft. Wir kommen gern wieder!!

Good location, good facilities even though they choose prime time to close and clean them.  Washing machine took our money and jammed so only one working as other was full of bugs. Went to ask for money back and tell the lady she was so rude!!!!! Then asked to buy ice and again was so rude. Didn't buy ice, Walked off and told her to have a nice day! If you don't like where you work quit !!!

We were really disappointed with Apollo Bay Recreation Reserve. The plan was to stay for 3 nights but we left the same day we arrived after seeing the shower and toilet amenities — they were dirty, outdated, and poorly maintained. When we spoke to the lady on reception, who said she would speak to the manager and call me back to let me know if a refund was possible, but I never heard anything after that. Overall, a very disappointing experience and not what we expected based on the photos and description. Stay clear of this one!

Was there for one night. Online it says there is a camp kitchen but there was just a toaster, a microwave and a fridge. Could cook that night would have booked if I knew that. Please label it as a kitchenette online and not a campkitchen. Furthermore two of four BBQ were out of use and the other two were dirty. I called there to maybe get a little refund but there was no understanding at all for my situation. They just said that it was my foult for not knowing there is no stove or something like that and that this is normal. I have been camping a lot through Australia and this was my first time this has happened. But all in all location was nice and unpowered site was also alright. Facilities are not very good but for the price ok.

Busy little park, stayed on unpowered site along edge of oval. No water connections on these sites.

Sites were nice and wide there were some overnight groups and others who stayed longer.

Amenities were good and pretty clean  considering the number of people using them.

Short walk into main shopping strip from the camp.

Costal breeze on hot days was welcoming.

Bookings are a must as this place is at capacity in busy periods.

We will stay again if in the area.

We were excited to stay at Apollo Bay Recreation Reserve from the 14th–16th, but the experience was disappointing. We had booked a riverside campsite and provided our campervan dimensions during booking to ensure we would have a space that fitted our van, yet were allocated a site that could not accommodate it. When we raised this with reception, the staff member was rude and appeared irritated. We were told the park was “fully booked,” even though there were many empty riverside sites for both nights of our stay. We were then offered a “staff” site as the only alternative, which resembled a hard rubbish area. Instead of the riverside view we paid for, we were faced with rusty fences and trailers. Due to this experience, we will not be returning.

I really want to call this what it is, a showgrounds.
Its facilities, and general fell are that of a showgrounds. The bonus is the river, and beach access. We enjoyed our stay, all the amenities were really clean. The laundry was a bonus too.
Non powered camp site on the river and short walk to town. Amenities were simple, ample and clean. Great location and latest access  to Apollo bay tennis courts.
false advertisement of a river side view. Instead we get a wonderful view of the amenities block. Will not be back.
Reasonably priced, both powered and unpowered sites right on the Barham River. 15 minute walk to the Pub for dinner.

I’ve been here a few times over the years, and it’s great to see the place going through a revamp. Stayed in one of the new eco-tents recently and it was genuinely charming — a really nice upgrade. Amenities are clean and totally fine, and the location is unbeatable. A great spot to relax and enjoy Apollo Bay.

The Caravan Park at the Recreation Reserve is a little gem for the campers who want bang for their buck!|• spacious grassy sites|• peaceful surrounds with a large oval for games|• amenities are clean and tidy|• Camp kitchen is fantastic for those camping without a kitchen|• little creek to fish in |• just over the road from the beach|* Please Note - this beach is NOT a safe swimming beach, patrolled beach is back in main area of town.
Best and most affordable campground in Apollo Bay. Ongoing upgrades and welcoming staff will see this place become the premier destination in Apollo Bay.
Not bad for a footy oval clean warm shower and toilets. Stayed in October for a night in a camper van

I really do Not understand the latest mean comments. We had a good stay, bathrooms are a bit old but totally fine and clean. Great wifi, functional and clean camp kitchen and reasonable prices for an unpowered Site. Of course it is a bit muddy if it Rains all day but it is not bad.
Clean Amenities and Kitchen, friendly Staff und easy accessible.

An absolutely smelly mud pit. Not worth the price. Booked for two nights but lasted one night. The mud was everywhere - two out of three toilet blocks were closed so in the middle of the night the kids had to walk through mud to the other side of the park to go to the bathroom. Tried to get a refund for the unused night - refused but we still decided to pay for somewhere rather than stay here another night.

Horrible customer service. DO NOT GO IF IT RAINS! You will get bogged and be sleeping in mud. You have to walk across the whole park just to get to the bathrooms, that hardly ever get cleaned. 1/5 stars.
Absolute mud puddle. Two out of three toilet blocks don’t work. Walk through mud to go to bathrooms. Stay anywhere but here

When we arrived there was a large trailer parked across our allocate site so we couldn’t park there. As the office was closed we had to call the out of hours number. The lady on the phone was really helpful and nice, but we had to wait for her to contact someone from the campsite to tell us what to do. It was really inconvenient as we had a big driving day and really want to make some dinner and go to bed, but we had to sit in the car out of the rain waiting. We got told then we could move to a different site of our choosing.

The site we originally were allocated was next to bathrooms that were out of order and the main amenities were on the other side of the oval. So our new spot was in a much better position.

The bathrooms were ok, showers were nice and hot. I probably wouldn’t come back due to the experience we had

Really a poor experience all around here, and not what you’d expect for a paid caravan park. For the poor value and downright inconvenient amenities, I would have stayed anywhere else if I had known.

Misleading Campfire Policy: the website says campfires allowed, which you would think means you can have a fire on site; however 1) unlike other GOR rec reserves that explicitly (I.e., properly) communicate that you need to hire a special fire pit, this location failed to mentioned that, and 2) they don’t even have enough for the sites they sell spots for, as I checked in during office hours and they didn’t have any - keep in mind all but 2 unpowered sites were still vacant and I didn’t see any fires on in neighbouring sites…a waste of the firewood I bought based on their website information.

Rude Receptionist: when I asked about the campfire situation one woman was very condescending and unhelpful which immediately made me wary. No apologies and no attempts to help troubleshoot other alternatives.

Barebones camp kitchen: not even a sponge or spare spoon in sight! No stovetop/burners (bbq outside but..c’mon) as you’d expect and as I’ve used in other rec reserve sites. What’s the point of staying here for some reprieve from the road if I have to lug out my gear to cook anyways…

Unwelcoming/sketchy grounds: no lighting for paths at night (which is still by about 6:30/7pm this time of year), no sign-postings to navigate around the grounds (where is the ‘laundry room’!?). Most of the unpowered sites are across from a sports oval and it became obvious no one wants to stay here for that reason because it 1) is incredibly windy without cover, so your van will shake all night, and 2) until dark, you’ll hear constant yelling and thudding of the ball while games are active.

Toilets: Not well signposted, not visible in the dark, one fully out of order, and the other so dark inside I had to use a flashlight (no light switches that I could find). Seriously??

Overall very disappointing, not a good part of the GOR, and really put a damper on part of my trip while here. Would not recommend. 👎
Great place for camping kids and pet friendly, nice view
Really lovely spot, enjoyed the river spot and the reception is so accommodating and generally so nice
Great basic camping experience

We have just spent 3 nights, our  site was good and close to a beautiful little creek that goes through the property.  The Staff are friendly and helpful which is wonderful.  The cleanliness of the amenities leaves a lot to be desired.  The cleaner arrived in the golf cart with the mop dragging in the dirt behind him, and proceeded to hose the amenities out, that is as close as it got to being cleaned.  The mop and bucket that is left in the ladies is filthy and not fit to be used.  The shower drains are full of hair and yuk, the drain in one shower is blocked completely and the water flows back through the amenities to a large drain in the main door.  Some showers are just a dribble, definitely one place I wore my thongs in the shower.
Unfortunately some people arrive with their children and seem to think they don't need to be parents while they are there and let them run wild, spoiling other people's days that don't want them running through their camp sites, kicking soccer balls up against expensive Motorhomes / Caravans and in general just being a nuisance.  There is a great big oval on the Reserve where they can play all the games they want without damaging other people's property, is not that we don't like children it is the irresponsible parents that are the problem.  The reserve has some good points but we would hesitate to stay here again.
Great spot close to town, can get busy during the peak season, plenty of facilities, i would stay here again.

We stayed in a cabin here with the family. For people wanting to do it on a budget it is well worth it.|It’s a clean park with good amenities. Has a BBQ area. Located on a reserve which is very peaceful.|The family enjoyed it. We will be back again next year.

Terrible new management under this ocean rd authority.
No idea of customer service. Turning people away when park is half empty and overcharging others.
Nice place, amenities need a bit of updating but not bad. Non powered camp sites next to the river were so small.

Wonderful place to stay. Had great support from the management specially from Karim who helped us going through our car breakdown and arranging the car and camper parked for towing. Good facilities and clean aminities. Will return again.

Lovely campsite overall! The facilities were abundant so even in peak season there were no lines for the bathroom. The camp kitchen was very spacious and had lots of utensils (toasters, kettles, etc). The site we had was quite small however we still fit and it was lovely and grassy and near a beautiful creek that looked onto a paddock with lots of cute farm animals. The location was great, very close to the town centre.
Amazing camp ground, sites by the river are exceptional such a peaceful trip. Kind staff, and amenities are all you need.

We found this place after disappointment elsewhere during the Xmas break. What a beautiful little find. It's excellently located close to the town centre (15 mins walk). Although it's close to town, the nature around is great (a few Koalas hidden at the back). I recommend trying to get a spot close to the river. The facilities are basic, but clean enough. If you're looking for something simple, rural, but close to the city and great value for money, worth checking out.

Just out of town. Situated at the rear of the football oval alongside a flowing creek. Good clean amenities block. Great views of the paddocks and hills.
Easy access for larger caravans.

My partner and I are currently staying here with our rooftop tent at a riverside unpowered site and could not be happier. Amanda at front check in is lovely and helpful, camp grounds are lovely and well maintained, the amenities are top notch clean and other campers very quiet & respectable. Will definitely be coming back thank you
Close to beach....amenities are decent..
Best spot to stay and chill out.  Plenty to do and see with the family
Love this place to bits. Been coming here for years now.
Great location.
It's not a recreation reserve.  Deceptive, misleading name.
Quite expensive for a solo traveller.  Unpowered site.  Self-contained vehicle $40 per night.

It’s a good park to stop for an overnight stay. We stayed a few nights. If I had my time again, I’d probably book at Marengo for a longer stay. ||Many sites under maintenance, so the park should be amazing once this upgrade is complete. Nice and close to town. Loved the visit from a koala.||Could do with an upgrade of facilities.
Great location on the river (site 12 riverside) We couldn’t be more grateful for Amanda’s great service and kindness. 10/10.
This Caravan Park is in the midst of being revamped. It is centred around a football oval but the location works just 10mins walk out of ApolloBay and close to the beach. Very clean and well run!
Park very tired.
We had a short, but so far very pleasant stay. The washrooms were clean. The grounds were beautifully laid out. Have nothing to complain about.
Good camping spot

I recently stayed at the Recreation Reserve over the weekend and had a fantastic experience. The amenities were top-notch, providing the perfect relaxation after a long day of touring. The overall atmosphere was welcoming, and I'm definitely considering coming back in the future. Highly recommended for a rejuvenating getaway!

Great place to stay, close to town and beach.
Staff friendly and helpful.
My partner are I booked her last minute on the day with some help. Lovely spot, quiet and right on the river. Lovely clean showers and toilets. Just a 5 min walk to the back beach.
They were very friendly, gave us tips where to see koalas and it was a good price for just a stay without electricity/water. I can recommend the camping ground !
Ein wenig in die Jahre gekommen aber ansonsten gut. Perfekte Lage direkt an der Great Ocean Road.

We spent one night there, and it was our worst night of the trip for us.
A group of young people from the bay next door were partying until 3am and we couldn't sleep, we tried calling the emergency number at reception for someone help us with this, but nothing was done to stop them (because the rules when you book says no noise is allowed after 10pm).

We tried to talk the group into stopping at around 1am, but we were unsuccessful. They continued until 3am and we have a plan to left at 6am 🫠

So if you are thinking to stop to rest because of the long drive, it's better to review, as you wouldn't have support if you need something similar.

Toilets are hit and miss depending who's staying.
Poo on toilet seats on final night

In general not a bad campground it has the basics of everything you'd need whether in a tent or a van, very close to town 2 min drive or if you're up for a walk.
The river that runs the length of the reserve was nice to walk along try your luck with a rod you might catch something or take a wander along the bushy side and try to spot a koala.
The facilities were in generally good order but outdated and could do with some TLC.
Has the potential to be a really nice camping spot it just needs that little bit extra

Thoroughly enjoyed the backdrop to our tent site was a quiet river and a herd of grazing cows. Something to look at when you open up your tent in the morning! The campsite facilities were dated but clean. Only two barbecues for the entire campsite, one of which didn't work...

Nice van park. Close to a beach. Decent facilities. Friendly owners?/caretakers?
Our dog was being a little psycho so we left early but would definitely stay again - minus dog...
Beautiful spot well maintained old but clean facilities

Freundliches Personal
Saubere Toiletten und Küche
Teuer / 52AUD für 1Nacht ohne Strom
The park was good but the town doesn't accommodate for caravans travelling through. No room to park so you can look around

Phone not manned. Receptionist not the friendliest. You get given a map and that’s it, no welcome or direction. The campsite is ok. Not the prettiest back drop. Lots of older, some derelict vans. Even next to the river lots of fences falling down. Dog poo right next to our site on arrival. I wouldn’t rush to go back which is a shame. Good location. If warm weather would be fine.

Absolutely shocking, horrid experience - do not camp here. Nowhere except a tiny sign at the entrance (that is very easily missed) does it say no fires. Apparently there are some inside the office (we were told by a very rude staff member while cooking our breakfast), but we were asked to locate the map/booking information inside a safe OUTSIDE the office, so had no reason to enter it. Nowhere on the website, while booking, or on the papers given to us does it say no fires. We understand we aren't allowed to build our own (although you'd have no idea by the lack of signage), but our fire pit is entirely contained and raised off the ground. As we're in a tent and not a caravan, you can imagine how freezing cold it is at night without one. We don't have any way to cook our food either. We have been asked to stop or it will be confiscated. We will be asking for a full refund as it is just not possible for us to stay. Unless you have a caravan, I can't imagine why anyone would want to stay here.

Lovely campsite nestled on the river, woke to the sounds of kookaburras and spotted a koala. Quiet spot despite being a short walk from the restaurants/pubs in Apollo Bay. Clean facilities and good location for tourist spots on Great Ocean Road. Highly recommend!
Nice

I had a very bad time at this campsite, which is open to everyone and unsecured, and at $52 a night, it's a rip-off.
And I'm not even talking about the state of the toilets, where you can only close 15% of the doors.
And what can I say about the fact that our bag was searched for food and drink and one drink was stolen.
The only positive point is that the campsite is close to everything, but when you don't feel comfortable leaving your things, you quickly forget this one positive point.
Once again, $52 a night for this is theft.
Good but not for someone who likes to camp surrounded by nature and forest. This is more like a caravan park. The facility is fantastic but not to my taste.
Good place to watch the footy.
Amazing place well worth the visit.  Grab a creekside camp-site and try and see a platypus.

Called 5 times throughout the day and left messages to book in. My calls were never returned.
I thought I'd take a punt and showed up but the office was closed without notice and no sign on the door explaining.
Drove through to see if I could park somewhere and just pay them the next day but all the sites were muddy & poorly kept and the amenities were terrible.
Went up the road to Marengo and the service was great (and cheaper somehow).
Was left scrambling around at the end of the day for a place to stay due to their poor management.
Good place, beautiful scenery & River Friendly maintenance staff , lots of activities to do open spaces
Great place to camp. Close to the beach. Can get a bit wet in winter.  Busy on footy days.
Just looking for a camping 🏕  spot  the girl  behind the desk  just  great 👍

Long weekend camp with the family on a wet, muddy and cold days as expected. Don’t ask me why but it was fun and not our first time as well. Set up our tents on a lush lawns while raining, had our small dogs with us since it’s a dog friendly place. Then it became muddy as we kept walking up and down on the watery lawns.

This campsite is along the Bahram river with plenty of outdoor activities. We did kayaking on the river across and under the bridge, fishing across the road and onto the beach we cast a line, walked, ran, swam and simply sat on the camping chair to watch the water. The campsite has friendly staff and a special mention to Chris who has been an amazing attendant to all my silly queries. Facilities include bbq and kitchen, toilet and bathrooms are decent, clean and bright (not yucky), coin laundry and outdoor washing area. Very happy with our stay and will recommend to our friends.

It is about 15 min walk to the jetty and break water if you want to go hard fishing. There is a seafood restaurant close-by which was awesome but ran out of oysters which I came for! It’s an informal outdoor setting but decent dining overlooking the bay. The town have strips of shops and park across the road with playground and bbq for family. But it was so packed and busy had to past it as there was no parking anymore.

They gave us a muddy spot, we got stuck and they didn’t even try to help us. We almost missed our flight, hopefully there were other campers to help us. Amenities were not worth the price. You should probably just park without paying because it will change nothing.
